questions.question: Explain the importance of land revenue for the British administration in India.
questions.answer: Land revenue was the main source of income for the British government in India. It helped them run administration and maintain their rule.

questions.question: Describe the main features of the Permanent Settlement.
questions.answer: The Permanent Settlement fixed the land revenue permanently and made zamindars responsible for collecting revenue from peasants.

questions.question: Explain the role of zamindars in the land revenue system.
questions.answer: Zamindars collected land revenue from peasants and paid a fixed amount to the British government.

questions.question: Explain the difficulties faced by peasants under the British revenue system.
questions.answer: Peasants had to pay high taxes and often borrowed money from moneylenders, which led to debt and poverty.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between zamindars and peasants.
questions.answer: Zamindars collected taxes from peasants and controlled the land, while peasants cultivated the land and paid rent.

questions.question: What was indigo and why was it important in colonial trade?
questions.answer: Indigo was a plant used to produce blue dye which was exported to European markets.

questions.question: Explain how indigo cultivation affected Indian farmers.
questions.answer: Farmers were forced to grow indigo instead of food crops and were paid very low prices for it.

questions.question: Describe the advance system used in indigo cultivation.
questions.answer: Planters gave advance loans to peasants and forced them to grow indigo crops in return.

questions.question: Why did peasants oppose indigo cultivation?
questions.answer: Indigo farming gave little profit and reduced the land available for food crops.

questions.question: Explain the causes of the Indigo Revolt.
questions.answer: Peasants revolted because they were forced to grow indigo and were treated unfairly by European planters.

questions.question: Describe how peasants protested during the Indigo Revolt.
questions.answer: Peasants refused to grow indigo and organised protests against the planters.

questions.question: Explain the role of moneylenders in rural society during British rule.
questions.answer: Moneylenders provided loans to peasants but charged high interest rates, leading to debt.

questions.question: Explain how British land revenue policies affected rural society.
questions.answer: These policies changed land ownership patterns and increased the burden on farmers.

questions.question: Explain why indigo cultivation was mainly carried out in Bengal.
questions.answer: Bengal had suitable soil and climate conditions for growing indigo plants.

questions.question: Describe the working conditions of indigo farmers.
questions.answer: Farmers had to work under pressure from planters and received very little profit.

questions.question: Explain the role of the Indigo Commission.
questions.answer: The Indigo Commission investigated the problems faced by indigo farmers after the revolt.

questions.question: Explain how indigo cultivation affected food production.
questions.answer: Farmers had less land for food crops because large areas were used to grow indigo.

questions.question: Explain how peasants resisted British agricultural policies.
questions.answer: Peasants protested, refused cultivation and organised revolts such as the Indigo Revolt.

questions.question: Explain the impact of the Indigo Revolt on colonial policies.
questions.answer: The revolt forced the British government to investigate planter practices and reduce exploitation.

questions.question: Explain how British policies changed rural economic life in India.
questions.answer: British revenue policies created new land systems and increased financial pressure on farmers.

questions.question: Explain the importance of studying indigo cultivation in Indian history.
questions.answer: It helps us understand colonial exploitation and peasant resistance.

questions.question: Describe the relationship between colonial trade and agricultural production.
questions.answer: Colonial trade influenced farmers to grow crops that were profitable for European markets.

questions.question: Explain how colonial policies affected Indian agriculture.
questions.answer: Colonial policies focused on revenue collection and export crops rather than farmers’ welfare.

questions.question: Explain how peasant protests influenced colonial administration.
questions.answer: Protests forced the government to investigate policies and make some reforms.

questions.question: Explain the historical importance of the Indigo Revolt.
questions.answer: The Indigo Revolt showed the strength of peasant resistance against colonial exploitation.
